regulator: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) + AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India)

NAV.definition: NAV is the per-unit market value of a mutual fund scheme. It is calculated as: (Total Assets - Total Liabilities) / Total Units Outstanding.
NAV.calculation_frequency: Every business day after market close (typically declared by 11 PM)
NAV.simple_analogy: Think of NAV like the price of one share in the fund. If a fund has assets worth ₹100 crore and 10 crore units, each unit's NAV = ₹10.
key_points: Higher NAV does not mean expensive — it just means the fund has grown over time
A fund with NAV ₹500 is NOT costlier than one with NAV ₹10 — returns depend on growth rate, not NAV level
NAV changes daily based on the underlying portfolio's market value

SIP.definition: A method of investing a fixed amount regularly (weekly/monthly/quarterly) in a mutual fund scheme.
SIP.benefit: Rupee cost averaging — you buy more units when NAV is low, fewer when NAV is high, reducing average cost over time.
SIP.simple_analogy: Like a monthly RD (Recurring Deposit) in a bank, but invested in the market instead of fixed interest.

Expense Ratio.definition: Annual fee charged by the fund house to manage your money, expressed as a percentage of AUM.
Expense Ratio.sebi_cap: Direct plans: up to 1.05% for equity; Regular plans: up to 1.30% for equity (SEBI revised caps 2018)
Expense Ratio.impact: A 1% expense ratio on ₹1 lakh means ₹1,000 per year is deducted from your returns.

AUM.definition: Total market value of all investments managed by a mutual fund scheme or AMC.

Exit Load.definition: A fee charged when you redeem (sell) your mutual fund units before a specified period.
Exit Load.example: 1% exit load if redeemed within 1 year — means if you invested ₹10,000 and redeem within a year, ₹100 is deducted.

ELSS.definition: A type of equity mutual fund that qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act.
ELSS.tax_benefit: Investment up to ₹1.5 lakh per year qualifies for deduction under Section 80C

Riskometer.definition: SEBI-mandated risk label on every mutual fund scheme.
Riskometer.note: Every MF scheme must display its riskometer in all communications.

Direct vs Regular Plan.direct: You invest directly with the AMC — no distributor, lower expense ratio, higher returns over long term.
Direct vs Regular Plan.regular: You invest through a distributor/broker — higher expense ratio because distributor gets commission.
Direct vs Regular Plan.advice: For informed investors, Direct plans are better. For those needing guidance, Regular plans via a certified distributor are fine.

KYC.requirement: Mandatory for all MF investments. One-time process done via KRA (KYC Registration Agency).
KYC.process: Can be done online (eKYC) or offline at AMC/distributor office.

sub_categories.name: Large Cap Fund
sub_categories.definition: Minimum 80% in top 100 companies by market cap. Lower risk than mid/small cap.

sub_categories.name: Mid Cap Fund
sub_categories.definition: Minimum 65% in 101st-250th companies by market cap. Higher risk, higher potential returns.

sub_categories.name: Small Cap Fund
sub_categories.definition: Minimum 65% in 251st company onwards. Highest risk, highest potential returns.

sub_categories.name: Multi Cap Fund
sub_categories.definition: Minimum 25% each in large, mid, and small cap.

sub_categories.name: Flexi Cap Fund
sub_categories.definition: Minimum 65% in equities with no restriction on market cap allocation.

sub_categories.name: ELSS
sub_categories.definition: Tax saving equity fund with 3-year lock-in. 80% in equities.

sub_categories.name: Sectoral/Thematic Fund
sub_categories.definition: Invests in a specific sector (IT, pharma, banking). Higher concentration risk.

sub_categories.name: Index Fund
sub_categories.definition: Passively tracks an index like Nifty 50 or Sensex. Very low expense ratio.

sub_categories.name: Liquid Fund
sub_categories.definition: Invests in instruments maturing within 91 days. Safest debt fund, ideal for parking surplus cash.

sub_categories.name: Ultra Short Duration Fund
sub_categories.definition: Macaulay duration 3-6 months. Slightly higher returns than liquid funds.

sub_categories.name: Short Duration Fund
sub_categories.definition: Macaulay duration 1-3 years.

sub_categories.name: Corporate Bond Fund
sub_categories.definition: Minimum 80% in highest-rated corporate bonds (AA+ and above).

sub_categories.name: Gilt Fund
sub_categories.definition: Invests in government securities. Zero credit risk but has interest rate risk.

sub_categories.name: Aggressive Hybrid Fund
sub_categories.definition: 65-80% equity, 20-35% debt. Good for moderate-risk investors wanting equity exposure with some stability.

sub_categories.name: Conservative Hybrid Fund
sub_categories.definition: 10-25% equity, 75-90% debt. For risk-averse investors wanting small equity exposure.

sub_categories.name: Balanced Advantage Fund / Dynamic Asset Allocation
sub_categories.definition: Dynamically shifts between equity and debt based on market valuations. Popular with first-time equity investors.

sub_categories.name: Retirement Fund
sub_categories.definition: 5-year lock-in or till retirement age, whichever is earlier.

sub_categories.name: Children's Fund
sub_categories.definition: 5-year lock-in or till child turns 18.

sub_categories.name: Fund of Funds (FoF)
sub_categories.definition: Invests in other mutual fund schemes rather than directly in stocks/bonds.

sub_categories.name: International/Overseas Fund
sub_categories.definition: Invests in foreign equities. Provides geographical diversification.

long_term_capital_gain.tax_rate: 12.5% on gains above ₹1.25 lakh per year (revised — Finance Act 2024)
long_term_capital_gain.exemption: ₹1.25 lakh LTCG per financial year is exempt

equity_funds.dividend: Added to income and taxed at slab rate. TDS @10% if dividend > ₹5,000 in a year.

debt_funds.definition: Funds with <65% in equities (invested on or after 1 April 2023)
debt_funds.tax_treatment: All gains (regardless of holding period) taxed as per investor's income tax slab rate.
debt_funds.note: Indexation benefit removed for debt funds purchased on or after 1 April 2023.

elss.gains_on_redemption: Taxed as LTCG at 12.5% above ₹1.25 lakh (same as equity funds)

how_to_invest: Choose between Direct or Regular plan
Select AMC and scheme based on your goal and risk appetite
Invest via AMC website, MF Central, or AMFI-registered distributor
For SIP: set up auto-debit mandate via NACH or UPI AutoPay
disclaimer: Mutual fund investments are subject to market risks. Past performance is not indicative of future returns. Read all scheme-related documents carefully before investing.
